docs.title: MoreTyping
docs.text: More types, for Python.

docs.title: Categories
docs.text:
1. Data
moretyping.data provides custom data structures or data processors designed to make storage/parsing easier.
  1. Visualise
moretyping.vis provides custom view classes — one-time-use class instances, easily convertible to string values, that provide different ways of representing or visualising data

docs.title: moretyping.data
docs.text: Provides custom data structures or data processors designed to make storage/parsing easier.

docs.title: APIs
docs.text: • Number(value: Numeric | Any)

docs.title: Annotations
docs.text:
type Numeric = float | int

class Number(metaclass=ProcessMeta, output_classes=(int, float)):
    def __new__(cls, value: Numeric | Any) -> Numeric:
        ...

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: value: Numeric | Any An integer or float, or any object convertible to a float.

docs.title: Return Value
docs.text: Returns Numeric — the integer/float created from value

docs.title: Raises
docs.text:
ValueError

If the value can't be cast to a numeric type.

docs.title: Details
docs.text:
If the value is an integer, return it unchanged.

Otherwise, return float(value).

docs.title: Example usage
docs.text:
from moretyping.data import Number

a = 8
b = "4"
c = "4.2"

print(Number(a))  # 8
print(Number(b))  # 4.0
print(Number(c))  # 4.2

docs.title: moretyping.vis
docs.text: Provides custom view classes — one-time-use class instances, easily convertible to string values, that provide different ways of representing or visualising data

docs.title: APIs
docs.text:
• VisLink(data: list[Any])
  • ViewJSON(data: Optional[Any])

docs.title: Annotations
docs.text:
class ViewJSON(metaclass=VisualiseMeta):
    def __init__(self, data: Optional[Any] = {}) -> None:
        ...

    @cached_property
    def string(self) -> str:
        ...

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: data: Optional[Any] Any JSON-compatible object.

docs.title: Return Value
docs.text: Returns a ViewJSON instance.

docs.title: Properties
docs.text: string: str A JSON-serialised verison of the data.

docs.title: Methods
docs.text:
def __str__(self) -> str:
    ...
Equivilant to the string property 
def __repr__(self) -> str:
    ...
Class name + stringified data.

docs.title: Details
docs.text: For any object, returns the JSON conversion of it.

docs.title: Example usage
docs.text:
from moretyping.vis import ViewJSON

data = {
    "users": [
        {
            "name": "User123",
            "age": 24,
        }
    ]
}

view = ViewJSON(data)

print(view)
#{
#   "users": [
#       {
#           "name": "User123",
#           "age": 24
#       }
#   ]
#}

docs.title: Annotations
docs.text:
class VisLink(metaclass=VisualiseMeta):
    def __init__(self, data: list[Any]) -> None:
        ...

    @cached_property
    def strdata(self) -> Iterable[str]:
        ...

    @cached_property
    def string(self) -> str:
        ...

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: data: list[Any] A list of objects of any kind.

docs.title: Return Value
docs.text: Returns a VisLink instance.

docs.title: Properties
docs.text: strdata: Iterable[str] An iterable object (not list) containing a stringified version of each element in data. string: str A stringified version of the data.

docs.title: Methods
docs.text:
def __str__(self) -> str:
    ...
Equivilant to the string property 
def __repr__(self) -> str:
    ...
Class name + stringified data.

docs.title: Details
docs.text: For a list such as [1, 2, 3], it will return [ 1 > 2 > 3 ].

docs.title: Example usage
docs.text:
from moretyping.vis import VisLink

data = [1, 2, 3, 4]

view = VisLink(data)

print(view)        # [ 1 > 2 > 3 > 4 ]
print(repr(view))  # <VisLink object> [ 1 > 2 > 3 > 4 ]
